sections.paragraph:
f. a word altogether poet.; prop. a lifting up, that which is erect (from the root יָשָׁה); hence-

(1) aid (compare Arab. وسى Conj. III. to aid, to comfort, properly to lift up). Job 6:13, מִמֶּנִּי תּוּשִׁיָּה נִדְּחָה “aid fled from me” (in the other member there is עֶזְרָה. LXX. βοήθεια). Pro 2:7 (LXX. σωτηρία), Mic 6:9 (at least in several MSS. and Verss.), Job 30:22 קרי.

(2) counsel (properly, the raising of any thing, that which any one wishes to raise or set up). Job 5:12, לֹא תַעֲשֶׂינָה יְדֵיהֶם תּוּשִׁיָּה “and their hands do not perform (their) counsel.” Vulg. quod cœperant.

(3) . counsel, i.q. wisdom. Job 11:6, כִּפְלַיִם לְתוּשִׁיָּה “the double of wisdom.” Job 12:16, עֹז וְתוּשִׁיָּה “might and counsel;” 26:3 Pro 3:21 8:14 18:1 Isa 28:29, הִפְלִא עֵצָה הִגְדִּיל תּוּשִׁיָּה “he is wonderful in counsel and of great wisdom.”
